curated_by: hand-curated from checked-in artifacts (see sources[]); not a fresh on-host run — every number traces to a shipped artifact or a primary source. The host ceiling that blocks a same-host fresh tuned-engine run is recorded under host_ceiling[], mirroring tools/fanout_longctx_probe.py.
what_this_is: The apples-to-apples peer column for the fanbench shared-prefix lever. fanbench measures how much of the naive N-times prefix redundancy reuse removes (fak-vs-fak geometry) priced at provider prompt-cache multiples (modeled $). This file pins the THIRD axis the issue asks to keep separate: what a TUNED shared-prefix serving engine that already occupies the same lever does in wall-clock. It does NOT claim fanbench beats those engines.

three_columns_kept_separate.note: These three are reported side by side in docs/benchmarks/FANOUT-BENCH-RESULTS.md and never blended. Reference cell: N=256 sub-agents.

col_c_tuned_engine_wall_clock.status: MEASURED on the engine's own regime (the peer column below)
col_c_tuned_engine_wall_clock.claim: These engines occupy the SAME prefix-reuse lever. fanbench does not out-throughput them; on the one CPU shared-prefix point measured head-to-head, llama.cpp is AHEAD of fak (preliminary). See peers[].

peers.mechanism: unified KV cache (kv_unified=true) + llama_memory_seq_cp — prefill the shared prefix once, share its cells into all C sequences (no copy-on-write), then batched-decode
peers.head_to_head_direction: llama.cpp AHEAD of fak on this CPU shared-prefix point (17.2 vs 5.2 agents/s at C=32). fanbench makes no claim to beat it.
peers.kv_memory_behavior: one unified KV arena; prefix cells shared across sequences (not duplicated). seq_cp shares cells with no COW — fak's Clone deep-copies instead, which costs fak performance but buys per-agent independent eviction of a poisoned span.
peers.cache_hit_behavior: prefill cost flat in C (the shared prefix is computed once); confirms prefix-once + share + batched decode.
peers.status: PRELIMINARY, settings-dependent, OPEN — single-config probe on non-default settings (kv_unified off by default; seq_cp asserts is_full on a partial buffer in this build). Not a settled conclusion. Needs multi-config + larger-scale + real llama-server parallel-slot verification.

peers.mechanism: radix tree of token sequences + longest-prefix match + LRU-leaf eviction; discovers every shared subtree automatically (incl. mid-run divergence), prefills a byte-identical prefix once and reuses it across requests

shape.workload: agents (ReAct: C concurrent agents share a system prefix) — the fanout shared-prefix topology
shape.metric: cache hit rate (hw/model-independent) + live prefill speedup

measured_fak_reproduction.note: fak runs the IDENTICAL radix-tree + longest-prefix + LRU-leaf algorithm (internal/radixkv) and lands inside SGLang's 50-99% hit band on the same workload shapes; the hit rate is a property of (workload, matching algorithm), hardware-independent.

peers.regime_caveat: SGLang is a GPU throughput engine; fanbench runs batch-1 on CPU. The fair, hardware-independent axis is cache hit rate, on which fak matches SGLang. fanbench does NOT claim to out-throughput SGLang in raw tok/s.

peers.mechanism: PagedAttention block-level hashing — KV blocks of a shared prefix are deduplicated and reused across requests automatically
peers.status: NOT RUN ON THIS HOST (SERVER_UNAVAILABLE) — no prefix-caching server reachable (127.0.0.1:8000). Recorded as a ceiling, never extrapolated. Mirrors the vllm-sglang skip in tools/fanout_longctx_probe.py.

host_ceiling.detail: llama-cli/llama-bench/llama-server + llama_cpp 0.3.30 present, but no small GGUF on host to drive bench_llamacpp_shared_prefix.py for a fresh fanout-shaped point; the checked-in C-sweep above is the existing measured Axis-4 probe.
no prefix-caching server reachable (127.0.0.1:8000 / :30000); no GPU VRAM on host. The SGLang column above is the existing shipped radixbench head-to-head.

honesty_line: No row here implies fanbench beats an engine that already shares the byte-identical prefix. The llama.cpp point has the tuned engine AHEAD of fak (preliminary); the SGLang point is a hit-rate match, not a throughput win; vLLM was not run. The fanbench win is reuse-vs-reprefill / vs-stateless (col A geometry, col B modeled $), not vs-tuned-engine wall-clock.
